title: Add JSON schema for Helm chart values
summary: Review #2 after addressing external review feedback from berendt. All four items (W-001: positive tests, W-002: rbac/webhook conditional constraint, I-001: regex exponent+suffix fix, I-002: chart version bump) are correctly implemented. All 111 helm-unittest tests pass, helm lint is clean, and schema validation is verified end-to-end via helm template. Existing tests in 5 sibling test files were correctly updated to satisfy the new schema constraint by adding webhook.enabled=false wherever rbac.namespaceScoped=true was already set.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (111 tests, 10 suites)
Edge cases covered (exponent+suffix rejection, boundary values, conditional constraint)
Positive tests verify valid non-default values pass schema validation
Negative tests verify invalid values produce clear error messages
Existing tests in sibling files updated for new schema constraint

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing Helm chart and test patterns
Schema uses shared $ref definition for resourceQuantity (DRY)
Test sections organized by requirement ID with comment headers
if/then conditional is well-structured with required guards

security_checklist.item: additionalProperties: false on all object levels prevents property injection
Regex patterns restrictive — no shell metacharacters or injection vectors

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible — minimal JSON Schema additions
Conditional constraint uses standard JSON Schema if/then (no custom extensions)
Defense in depth preserved — template-level fail directive remains alongside schema enforcement

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code — resourceQuantity shared via $ref

fail_fast_checklist.item: Schema validation catches errors before template rendering
Conditional constraint rejects invalid rbac+webhook combo at schema level

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs validated via schema types, enums, ranges, patterns
if condition uses required guards to prevent false matches

issues_found.description: Documentation at docs/reference/backend/helm-values-schema.md does not reflect three changes from this diff: (1) the rbac section (line 60-62) doesn't mention the new conditional constraint requiring webhook.enabled=false when rbac.namespaceScoped=true, (2) the resource quantity pattern shown on line 49 was already a simplification but now also omits the exponent+suffix mutual exclusion, (3) the test coverage table (lines 131-139) doesn't list the new positive tests or the conditional constraint tests. The chart version bump to 0.2.0 is not documented either.
issues_found.fix: Update the docs to reflect: (1) add a 'Conditional Constraints' section documenting rbac.namespaceScoped=true requires webhook.enabled=false, (2) update the regex pattern on line 49 to match the actual schema pattern, (3) add rows to the test coverage table for positive tests and conditional constraint tests.
issues_found.evidence: Line 49 shows pattern ^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?(m|k|M|G|T|P|E|Ki|Mi|Gi|Ti|Pi|Ei)?$ but schema has ^(\.[0-9]+|[0-9]+(\.[0-9]*)?)((e[0-9]+)|(m|k|M|G|T|P|E|Ki|Mi|Gi|Ti|Pi|Ei))?$. Line 62 shows rbac.namespaceScoped with no constraint column entry, but the schema now enforces webhook.enabled=false when true.

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a positive test for exponent-only resource quantities (e.g., cpu: '1e3') to complement the negative tests for exponent+suffix combinations.
next_steps: Update docs/reference/backend/helm-values-schema.md to reflect the conditional constraint, corrected regex pattern, and expanded test coverage.
